sections.title: Talking to either Ennius or Furia Tullus
Talking to either again after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey I recognise you two! You were part of the delegation to Varrock!

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: The Prince is waiting for you. You'll find him in the temple to the south, on the other side of the bazaar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Is this the one?

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: I believe so.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Doesn't look like much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey I recognise you two! You were part of the delegation to Varrock!

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Indeed we were.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: More than part of. We organised the whole thing!

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: As was our duty.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: The Prince could have contributed.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Do not air your dirty laundry in public, brother.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Hmph!

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: You. The Prince wishes to speak with you. He awaits you at the temple.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I find this temple?

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: On the other side of the bazaar, south of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. Guess I should get going then.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... I'll leave you to it.

sections.title: Attempting to open the gate in the temple basement before talking to Metzli
Talking to Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
Talking to Metzli before asking about Prince Itzla

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: You have returned.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: The Prince awaits you down in the crypt.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: I know your face. I have seen it a thousand times before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... I think you must have me confused with someone else. We've never met.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Yes... and no. This is our first encounter, and yet I know you better than most. You wear the face of my only true companion. You wear the face of death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your only companion is death? You should probably get out more.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: You jest, as your kind often do. Is that how you drown out their screams, I wonder? Is that how you hide your pain?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well things got deep very quickly. Is this how you normally introduce yourself to someone?

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Few seek an introduction with the Sect of Ranul. They spend their days in Ralos' light. The darkness hides an inevitable truth that they prefer to shy away from.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... So who actually are you?

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: I am Metzli, Teokan of Ranul. You I already know. You are the one that saved the life of my counterpart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Teokan? So there are two of you?

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Yes. As Teokan of Ranul, I lead the Sect of Ranul. My counterpart, Servius, leads the Church of Ralos.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm meant to be meeting Prince Itzla here.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Yes you are. He is speaking with Servius, down in the crypt. Not a place you'd normally find the Teokan of Ralos, but the Prince desired the privacy only Ranul can offer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better head down there.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Farewell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about the Church of Ralos?

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Those who worship the light can be found throughout these lands, and it is the Church of Ralos that leads their worship.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: If you wish to learn more, you should seek out Servius or one of his Teoki. They will be better placed to guide you than I, for my duty is one of darkness, not light.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about the Sect of Ranul?

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Ralos' light can be found all across Varlamore, but when that light fades, there is only one who can guide us through the darkness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ranul?

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Yes. You will not find many worshippers of Ranul in your travels. Indeed, many choose to hide from him their whole lives, but no matter what, we will all meet Ranul in the end.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: When the sun sets, it is he that will guide us. He will lead us through the Gates of Mictl to where we may finally rest. The duty of the Sect of Ranul is to ensure that all are prepared for that journey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Farewell.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Itzla or Servius
Talking to either after hearing about the assassination attempt

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ah, you're back. Shall we get down to business?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a plan.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: [player name], head to the palace and find the Tullus twins. They'll know where you can find those knights.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right to it.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ah, there you are. Nilsal, adventurer. I don't believe you've properly met my father. This is Servius, the Teokan of Ralos and the man whose life you saved.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your father? I didn't realise you were related.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Yes, very fun stuff. All a bit of a convoluted mess as well! I'll be sure to tell you all about it at some point. For now though, [player name], meet Servius. Servius, meet [player name].

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Nilsal, my child. It is an honour to meet you. Were it not for you, I may well already be making my journey to Mictl. On behalf of both myself and Ralos, I thank you.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well let's not start celebrating just yet. There's a reason why we're lurking in this crypt.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Sorry about that, by the way. I'd have much rather given you a proper welcome on your arrival, but given the situation, it's probably best we take a bit more care.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not a problem. Although, I did have an interesting encounter on my way here.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ah, the Tullus twins. Fascinating pair, aren't they?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I was talking about Metzli. I met her upstairs.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Oh, you met her! My deepest sympathies.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Metzli and her sect play an essential role in our lives. You should not judge her so harshly.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: You're quite right, of course. But anyway, shall we get down to business?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a plan.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Kuani! So we have quite the predicament on our hands. My mother isn't too happy, oddly enough, and has tasked me with sorting it all out.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Someone wants our dear Teokan dead, and we have no idea who! All we know is that they were a Varlamorian.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A Varlamorian who had access to the mainland before the treaty was signed. Surely that limits the options a fair bit?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Right you are, [player name]! In fact, it limits them to just the members of our little delegation. In other words, myself, Servius, the Tullus twins and our retinue of knights.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Now I don't think it was me... and I'm guessing you didn't try to arrange your own assassination, Servius?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: I'd like to think I'd have done a better job if I had.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Too right. Very sloppy work. A top example of how not to assassinate someone. Still, probably best we track them down all the same.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So that leaves the Tullus twins and the knights.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Ennius and Furia are loyal children of Ralos. They would not wish to see my death.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Those knights should also be loyal children of Ralos, but I agree, they are far more likely candidates. Let us investigate them and see what we can find.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Servius, until we have this matter sorted, you should remain here in the capital. The palace will be the safest place.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Nonsense! I am the Teokan of Ralos and I have a duty to the people. I must return to the Teomat at once!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: The Teomat does not offer the protection of the palace. You'll be an easy target there.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: I am the chosen messenger of Ralos. He would not have me cower and hide. I will return to the Teomat.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well this is a terrible idea that's definitely going to blow up in all our faces later, but it seems you have made your decision. However, I insist that you let me personally accompany you.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: My child, I would be delighted to have you as a travelling companion.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: [player name], since Servius here seems keen on letting this killer have another go, you'll need to investigate the knights on your own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure I can manage that.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Kuani! Head up to the palace and find the Tullus twins. They dealt with all the fun admin work. They'll know where those knights can be found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get to it right away.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Then it sounds like we're all set. Timoiva, and may the sun light your way!

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Timoiva, my child.

sections.title: Talking to either again before getting the crest

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Take this crest. It will mark you as a representative of the Queen herself. Show it to the knights and ask them to return here.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Do you need something?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost that crest you gave me.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Oh good. So now there's a crest of the Queen out there somewhere that anyone could use. Why did the Prince pick this one?

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: We will have it dealt with. For now, take another. However, I suggest you take more care this time.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: It seems the Prince has new chores for us.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: You do not know that.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Why else would he have sent the adventurer back to us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You two are quite the pair, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: I do not believe that was a compliment, brother.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Oh... Well let's get to it then. What does the Prince need now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's asked me to investigate the knights who were part of the delegation to Varrock. He suspects one of them may have been involved in the plot to kill the Teokan.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Has he forgotten that he himself leads the Knights of Varlamore? Surely he'd be best placed to deal with any of their wrongdoings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's staying with Servius in case there's another attempt on his life.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: It is wise to keep the Teokan under close protection, and we are well placed to support this investigation.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: If you say so.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: The delegation consisted of six knights. Given recent events, I requested they make us aware of their activities. I suggest you seek them out and ask them to return here.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Arrun and Claudia should be at the bazaar. Nel will be at the cothon. Velam and Azali are somewhere in Outer Fortis, likely at the pub. Mezan will be at the colosseum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if any refuse to come?

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: This crest will mark you as a representative of the Queen herself. Show it to them and they will not refuse.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Not unless they fancy revealing themselves as the traitor, which doesn't seem likely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find the bazaar?

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Surely you passed through it on your way to the temple earlier. It's directly south of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find the cothon?

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: From here, just take the eastern path past the museum. Once you reach the water, you have found the cothon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Outer Fortis?

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: It's the part of the city outside the main walls. Head south from here and you'll find it on the other side of the gates.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Head to the pub once you get there. That's likely where Velam and Azali are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find the colosseum?

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: On the cliffs overlooking the city, east of here. I'd be concerned if you couldn't find it. It's pretty much impossible to miss.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Mezan will likely already be inside. I'd suggest searching the training area for him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

sections.title: Talking to Arrun or Claudia after speaking to them once

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Do you have the amulet?

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Do you have the amulet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Best you get to it then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it right here. That man in blue over there had it.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Good work. He'll lead us right to those thieves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'll return to the palace now?

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: We'll be there once the thieves are dealt with. It won't take us long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well good luck then.

sections.title: Talking to either again after giving the amulet

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. Do you know how long it will be before you can return to the palace?

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Not long. We'll be there as soon as the thieves are dealt with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. Good luck with them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Nilsal. Can we help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ennius and Furia Tullus need you to return to the palace.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: If that were so, you'd have proof.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, well... I... I don't.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Thought so. Be off with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it just so happens I have this.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Hmm... Well we're not going anywhere until our work here is done. If you want us back at the palace, you'd best help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can do that. What do you need?

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: A band of thieves has been active here recently, but we came up with a way to track them down. We planted a nice shiny amulet on one of the stalls. Sure enough, it was stolen soon after.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Find the amulet and tell us who was carrying it. We can then follow them right to the rest of their group.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How will I find it?

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Beat them at their own game. Go looking through a few pockets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well this doesn't sound totally orthodox, but fair enough. I'll be back soon.

sections.title: Searching the correct crate before talking to Nel
Talking to Nel after speaking to her once

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Any luck finding that crate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. Could you remind me what I'm looking for?

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: The crate should be on the docks rather than on a ship. It's of a standard size and isn't part of a stack. It's directly next to two barrels of fish and opposite a bench.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. I'll keep looking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I found it. The ship it's from is called The Fortis Spark.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Perfect! That's my work here done then. I'll return to the palace shortly. Timoiva!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great!

sections.title: Talking to her again after finding the crate

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Hello again. I'll be returning to the palace shortly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great!

sections.title: Searching any crate after Nel's work is done
Searching any crates after Nel's work is done

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I'm here on behalf of Ennius and Furia Tullus. They need you to return to the palace immediately.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: You're working for the Tullus twins? Do you have proof?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... No.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Well rules are rules, I'm afraid. Without proof, I can't take any orders from you.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Huh... Fair enough. I'm happy to return to the palace, but I can't until I've finished my work here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it you're doing? Maybe I can help get it done faster.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: With the recent opening of the kingdom, the cothon has become much busier. Good for business of course, but not so good when it comes to smugglers.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: We've had a tip-off that there's a ship here involved in weapons smuggling, but we don't know which one.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: If you can find the crate of weapons and tell me what ship it's from, I'll be able to return to the palace much faster.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds easy enough.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: You say that now, but there's no shortage of crates around here. Thankfully, the tip-off did give us some information that might help you find it.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: The crate should be on the docks rather than on a ship. It's of a standard size and isn't part of a stack. It's directly next to two barrels of fish and opposite a bench.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good to know. I'll get to it and let you know what I find.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Are you ready to train? Remember, bring two attack styles in with you. It will help me test my faith in Ralos.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Ralos will defend me!

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Ralos' light protects me!

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Shield me, Ralos!

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: You fought well in there. I should train with you more often.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'll return to the palace now?

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Yes, I'll head over there shortly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perfect! Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Mezan after he agrees to return to the palace

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Nilsal, adventurer. I'll be returning to the palace soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perfect! Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Sorry, but I'm waiting for someone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you might need to meet with them some other time. You're needed back at the palace.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: You don't work for the palace. Why would they send you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working directly for the Prince.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: If you are, then prove it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I can't.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Well then come back when you can.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Brand new here and already working for the Prince... Strange.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe, but like I say, you're needed at the palace.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: And I'll return there... eventually. First, I'm here for some training, but it seems my training partner is a no show.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Hmm... You look like you know a thing or two. You can take his place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can?

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Yes! Join me in the colosseum for some training. Once we're done, I'll return to the palace.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Oh, and bring two attack styles in with you. I want some variety, and it will help me test my faith in Ralos.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready. Let's do this.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Kuani!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to go and prepare.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: I'll be here once you're ready.

sections.title: Talking to Velam while Azali is following

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: I thought you were going to that fountain. It's just outside the gates to the east of here.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Ohh, this is tiring. I think I need a quick break.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Can we have a break? I'm feeling a bit iffy...

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Huh? That looks interesting...

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Wow! What's that over there?!

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Hey! You're going too fast!

sections.title: Talking to Azali while she's following you

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This way, Azali.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: This isn't the right way! I'm going back to the pub!

sections.title: After leading Azali to the fountain

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Oh boy...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Feeling a bit better now?

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Not exactly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it will have to do. You're needed over at the palace.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: This day is the worst. Fine, I'll head over there soon.

sections.title: Talking to Azali after sobering up

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll be on my way to the palace soon.

sections.title: Talking to Velam after Azali sobered up

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll be on my way to the palace soon.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Nilsal. Thanks for helping out with that little issue. I'll be heading over to the palace soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Well look who it is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... I don't know you.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: I told you not to have that last one. Look at you!

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Stop whining! I'm fine!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... The two of you have been asked to report to the palace as soon as you can.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Report? I don't think so! It's our day off!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay well... Oh... I was going to show you a crest, but I seem to have lost it.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Okay then! Timoiva for now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but I have this crest here that says otherwise.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Ah, now that's going to be a problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Azali here can't go showing up to the palace in this state. We need to sober her up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Where's the nearest fountain?

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: There's one outside the gates just to the east of here... But how does a fountain help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well there's nothing like a little dunk to sober you up a bit. Azali, come with me.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Should you not pick up your follower first?

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: Ohh... We're going somewhere new? How exciting!

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Did you find anything in the Kualti Headquarters?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Then you should keep looking.

sections.title: Searching a wrong chest, or the correct chest before and after the quest

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Did you find anything in the Kualti Headquarters?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a letter to one of the knights, but I haven't read it yet.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Then you should do so and see what it says.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a letter to one of the knights. It seems that Velam is our traitor.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: You are sure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The letter directed him to arrange the death of the Teokan. It also mentioned something called Twilight's Promise and something else called the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Then it sounds like Velam has some explaining to do. This should be fun.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Velam, step forward.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Velam, you have been found to be a traitor to the Kingdom of Varlamore.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: What? I don't understand!

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: We know you attempted to arrange the murder of the Teokan of Ralos. Do you have anything to say about this?

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: This isn't true! Someone must have set me up!

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Your lies mean nothing here. Your guilt has already been proven.

lines.speaker: Knight of Varlamore
lines.text: No! This isn't right!

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: For you, this is the only thing that's right.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: The rest of you are dismissed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You killed him!

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: What were you expecting? That we'd invite him to sit down for dinner and drinks? I don't know how they do things where you come from, but you're in Varlamore now. This is how we deal with traitors.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We should have questioned him! Now we have no idea who he was working for!

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Well it's done now, so little point dwelling on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Little point...

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: While my brother may have been a touch too hasty, he is right. What's done is done. You should return to the Prince and let him know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... Fine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Itzla and Servius said they were going to something called a Teomat.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Makes sense. The Teomat is the centre of all religion in Varlamore. The Teokan would have been eager to return to his home.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: You'll find the Teomat atop Ralos' Rise, but it's quite the walk from here. You should speak to Regulus Cento outside about a faster method of travel.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: You should not waste time. The Prince will be eager to hear what you've discovered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll go and see Regulus.

sections.title: Talking to the Tullus twins after starting the cutscene
Talking to the Tullus twins after finishing the cutscene

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You killed him!

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Speak to Regulus Cento outside about a faster method of travel. You'll likely need it if you wish to travel to the Teomat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll go and see him.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: Have you spoken to all the knights?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. They should be here soon.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: Six knights, but likely only one traitor. How do you intend to work out who it is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm going to guess asking nicely won't work.

lines.speaker: Furia Tullus
lines.text: After they were picked for the delegation, the knights were granted beds in the Kualti Headquarters, just west of here. I would suggest you search there for anything of note.

lines.speaker: Ennius Tullus
lines.text: We'll keep the knights busy until you're done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Nilsal, adventurer. Do you wish to return to Varrock?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was told to ask you about getting to the Teomat.

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Have you properly introduced yourself to Renu yet? She'll be able to take you right there!

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Have you properly introduced yourself to Renu yet? She'll be able to take you right there! Here, I'm sure this will help the two of you become firm iknamis.

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Nilsal, adventurer. Do you wish to return to Varrock?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was told to ask you about getting to the Teomat.

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Ah, kuani! Well you'll be pleased to know that Prince Itzla has already granted me permission to give you your very own quetzal, free of charge!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My own quetzal?

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: That's right! And I've already picked out the perfect one. This here is Renu. She finished training not too long ago and has been eagerly awaiting her first rider.

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Now, Renu here isn't as experienced as Primio, so don't expect her to be taking you too far. However, she's more than capable of flying anywhere in Varlamore, provided there's somewhere suitable to land.

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: You'll find that we already have plenty of landing sites all across the kingdom, and more are being built all the time. You could probably even build a few of your own if you wanted.

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Now, why don't you two properly introduce yourselves to each other. I have the perfect thing here to help with that.

sections.title: Talking to Regulus after feeding Renu

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Nilsal, adventurer. Do you wish to return to Varrock?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was told to ask you about getting to the Teomat.

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: You have everything you need to get there already. Renu will be able to take you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Renu. I have something for you here.

lines.speaker: Regulus Cento
lines.text: Kuani! I reckon she'll let you ride her now.

sections.title: Talking to Metzli before speaking to the prince

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: The Prince and my counterpart wish to speak with you. You should go to them at once.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Itzla without speaking to Regulus

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name]. How goes your investigation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking into those knights.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well keep at it. The Tullus twins should be able to help you where needed.

sections.title: Talking to Prince Itzla or Servius again

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: If you want to learn about the Final Dawn, Metzli would be the best person to speak to. She'll be around here somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll see if she can help me.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name]. Welcome to the Teomat.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Nilsal, my child.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: So, how did your investigation go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a letter to one of the knights, Velam. The letter instructed him to arrange the death of Servius.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Tetamo! One of my own knights! I will need to swiftly deal with this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well there's actually no need. Ennius killed him as soon as we found out.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Ah, excellent! Such should be the fate of all traitors.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But Ennius killed him before we could even question him!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Hmm, that's unfortunate. It would have been good to know who he was working for. No matter though! I'm sure we'll find another way of getting to the bottom of this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does it not bother you how quickly Ennius killed him? Is it not a bit odd?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Here in Varlamore, justice is delivered swiftly and harshly. Ennius may have taken that a bit too literally... but there's not much we can do about that now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But do you not find it suspicious? I find a letter perfectly implicating one of the knights, but he's then quickly killed before he can be questioned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems like a pretty good way of covering things up, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: The Tullus family have loyally served the rulers of Varlamore since the death of Emperor Imafore. For one of them to be a traitor is unthinkable!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: It's a dangerous accusation indeed. That said... the family does have a bit of a history.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: My family has not always ruled Varlamore. In fact, it was the Tullus family who reigned for the majority of the kingdom's history.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: That all ended when good old Imafore declared the kingdom to be his new empire. He celebrated by going on a nice little murder spree. One war later and dear old Imafore found himself without a head.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Had that not happened, the throne would have eventually passed down to... Furia, I think... I believe she's the slightly older of the two.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... And you don't think that might be a good reason for her and Ennius to be traitors?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Imafore's death was over a hundred years ago! His family had no love for him then, and they've loyally served the Arkans since.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Yes, very true, but [player name] also has valid concerns. We need to be careful of assumptions, but it seems it would be wise for us to keep an eye on the two of them from now on.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Now, [player name], did your investigation uncover anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The letter I found mentioned two things: Twilight's Promise and the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Twilight's Promise? I've never heard of that. I wonder if it has anything to do with the Twilight Emissaries...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Twilight Emissaries?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: A nice religious cult that has an unhealthy obsession with the end of the world. They've never caused any major problems before, so we've mostly left them be.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: However, given this revelation, perhaps it's time to pay a little closer attention to their dealings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And the Final Dawn... What's that?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: Nothing more than blasphemy! It is not something we should waste our time talking about.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: As you can see, the Final Dawn is a bit of a... controversial topic. It's a religious belief...

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: An unofficial religious belief!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: It's an unofficial religious belief concerning the end of the world. Oddly enough, our friends in the Twilight Emissaries are big fans of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Where can I learn more about the Final Dawn?

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: It's not something I care to know about, but if anyone around here were to know, it would be Metzli.

lines.speaker: Servius, Teokan of Ralos
lines.text: The Sect of Ranul are responsible for maintaining the library here. That includes dealing with any forbidden materials.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Metzli returned to the Teomat shortly before we did. She'll be around here somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll go and see if she can help me.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: We meet again. Not a chance encounter I see, for it is clear that you want something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was hoping you could help me with some information.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Quite possibly. The Sect of Ranul know better than most that time sees the end of all things. When we are gone, what remains? Knowledge must be passed on, lest it be lost forever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, what you're describing there is a book. I'm familiar with the concept.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: But what is a book without the right reader? Words can be misunderstood, misconstrued. That assumes those words were even right to begin with.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Think of all that we believe to know. All those words we have read. How many were the truth, I wonder.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, well before we go down another rabbit hole, I'm looking for information on the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: The Final Dawn? This is something I can help with, but first, to understand the Final Dawn, you must understand Ximoua.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ximoua? That's a fancy word.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: The word is a gift to us from those who walked these lands before we did. They knew the truths of this world... in a way we can only dream of.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: We call them the Old Ones. They understood the importance of passing on their knowledge. So much of what we know, comes from them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You speak of the Old Ones?

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Yes. They understood the importance of passing on their knowledge. So much of what we know, comes from them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... So what is Ximoua?

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Our world endures in a way we cannot comprehend, but like all things, it will not do so forever. Its light will one day die, but this is not something to be feared, it is something to be embraced.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: You see, we are part of a cycle. Something far larger than ourselves. When the time comes, Ralos and Ranul will come together as one, consuming these lands in holy fire, until there is nothing left but ash.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: But even in ash, embers remain. From those embers, a new world will be born, and the cycle will continue once more. This is Ximoua.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you think the world will one day end? That's quite something.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Not think. Know. This knowledge is a gift. One that was passed down to us from the Old Ones.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you said it yourself: Knowledge passed down can be misconstrued. How can anyone know the truth for sure? It's not like there are any of these Old Ones around for us to ask.

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: Indeed, and that is why this is just one interpretation. There are those out there with another: Tsak Tauli, the Final Dawn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what do they believe?

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: They do not just believe that the world will end. They believe it is their duty to make it happen. They see it as their singular purpose, and will stop at nothing to make it so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: These people... You speak of the Twilight Emissaries?

lines.speaker: Metzli, Teokan of Ranul
lines.text: It is said that they are amongst those with this belief.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I'd better...

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: [player name]! I need you outside, now! We have a little bit of an issue!

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: What happened?!

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: I'm sorry, my Prince, but we're not sure. None of the attackers made it inside, but Metzli has disappeared.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Metzli? Surely not...

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Thankfully the blade missed his heart, but we should get him to the capital as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Yes, of course. Ensure no time is wasted.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Of course, my Prince. We'll go at once.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well... this has been a bit of a mess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Word has been sent to mother about what happened here. While we wait to hear back from her, I've already been thinking about a potential plan for how we might go about dealing with our cultist problem.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: However, let's not worry about that just now. Instead, I wanted to thank you for all that you've done. You may be an outsider, but you've already proven yourself to be a true friend to Varlamore. Thank you, iknami.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's this plan you mentioned?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: You want to hear it already? I'd have thought you might have wanted a bit of a break from all the excitement, iknami.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair point. I'll be back once I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I'll be here, [player name].

sections.title: Talking to Prince Itzla Arkan after the guard leaves

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Well... this has been a bit of a mess.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Nilsal, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are things going?

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Father should make a full recovery, though given that his would-be killers are still out there, that's not anywhere near as much comfort as it should be.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: I've sent word to mother about what happened here, but I've not heard back yet. Once I have, I'll start planning our next move.

lines.speaker: Prince Itzla Arkan
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll be in touch soon enough. Until then, timoiva, and may the sun light your way.
